notes: Opening opportunity work notes. Objective: plaintiff opening for proposition that by May 8, 2026 11:59 PM ET, a message/note credibly reported to have been written by Jeffrey Epstein and intended as a suicide note/final message/equivalent had been made publicly available. Opportunity allows only record_opening_statement; no evidence submission. Inspected get_case/list_evidence and all five case-packet files via read_evidence_range. Evidence map: market-page preserves Polymarket rule requiring public availability by listed date, credible reporting of Epstein authorship, suicide/final/equivalent intent, and consensus credible reporting; disputed May 8 market status and API/page state captured 2026-05-12. official-source-record says rule specified no single official source, identifies SDNY Judge Kenneth Karas unsealing one-page note in Tartaglione case, but packet lacks local SDNY PDF/order/note image and says court did not authenticate authorship/chain. primary-evidence preserves Reuters/NPR/BBC/Guardian excerpts; helpful: multiple credible outlets before May 8 reported court release, described document as purported/alleged suicide note, quoted goodbye language; adverse: lack independent authentication/DOJ authenticity, no chain assessment, uncertainty over intent. secondary-reporting identifies NYT leads but no full capture. unresolved-record-gaps lists missing SDNY official court exhibits, NYT capture, and unresolved consensus/authorship/intent. Native investigation before opening: searched Reuters/NPR/BBC/Guardian/NYT/SDNY terms; found official SDNY order PDF at nysd.uscourts.gov/sites/default/files/2026-05/16cr832%20-%20Tartaglione%20Order%20-%2026.5.6.pdf. It says NYT sought access to a suicide note purportedly authored by Epstein, recovered by Tartaglione, submitted by Tartaglione attorneys, and court granted unsealing as a judicial document subject to public access. It does not authenticate authorship. Found PBS/AP article reporting May 6/7, Karas release, Tartaglione discovery claim, note language, and adverse caveats: unclear who wrote it, not in government reports, Epstein reportedly said Tartaglione tried to kill him. Found Guardian May 6 article reporting first public availability, quote, no signature, Guardian not verified, Epstein maintained not suicidal. Theory: plaintiff should not claim proven authorship. Winning theory: proposition/rule asks whether credible reporting said note was written by Epstein and intended as suicide/final/equivalent; admitted packet shows that credible reporting consensus existed before deadline, while caveats go to weight not dispositive failure. Defense likely argues hedging defeats credibility and intent. Response: wording is 'credibly reported to have been written' not authenticated; hedging is responsible reporting; public court release plus multiple credible reports and note language satisfy preponderance. Later evidence plan: submit SDNY order PDF, note image/court handout if obtainable, AP/PBS or AP original, Reuters, Guardian, NYT if accessible; preserve adverse DOJ/report authenticity caveats.

Opening opportunity openings:defendant. Record checked with get_case/list_evidence; five case-packet text files visible and no submitted evidence/exhibits beyond packet. Read full bytes for ev_90fa205907f7_market-page, ev_e631aed74f36_primary-evidence, ev_b3b39b867ad2_official-source-record, ev_d6c0d4659002_secondary-reporting, ev_2680dc66be4e_unresolved-record-gaps. Case theory: concede packet tends to show public release/unsealing before May 8, but defend on the full rule elements: credible reporting that the note was written by Epstein and intended as suicide note/final message/equivalent. Packet repeatedly preserves alleged/purported language, no independent verification, no official authentication, no chain/custody assessment, and uncertainty about meaning/intent. Native investigation for source targeting only: web searched SDNY/Tartaglione/Epstein note terms; found official SDNY order PDF at nysd.uscourts.gov/sites/default/files/2026-05/16cr832%20-%20Tartaglione%20Order%20-%2026.5.6.pdf. It appears useful for later admission: order grants NYT unsealing request as to alleged/purported note and directs docketing/unsealing of material placed in vault May 20, 2021, but does not authenticate authorship or intent. Other later evidence targets: official note image/docket entry, full Reuters/NPR/BBC/Guardian captures, NYT authentication follow-up, DOJ reference to note, handwriting/authentication analyses/corrections. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md. Opening will not cite unadmitted outside source as record proof.

Arguments opportunity work notes. Rechecked get_case/list_evidence before filing. Record change since opening: defendant opening added; no new evidence from defendant. Defendant concedes/public availability likely but attacks authorship, chain, intent. Evidence plan: admit strongest primary/near-primary and corroborating public reporting before relying on it. Native retrieval: downloaded SDNY official order PDF from court site to /aar-codex/sources-20260630-args/sdny-tartaglione-order-2026-05-06.pdf at 2026-06-30T04:33:49Z; size 128,992; sha256 7225441cb802084e2b9485ebc2c6d246fbfa08e109fe650cb14f445dc4745ca0. Local pdftotext unavailable; used web-rendered text extraction and preserved limitation. Submitted and accepted: SDNY official order extraction ev_0ed0ef50e84e_submitted-evidence-01-plaintiff-0ed0ef50e84e; PBS/AP relevant article extraction ev_757899113d89_submitted-evidence-02-plaintiff-757899113d89; Guardian relevant extraction ev_be7a5dd8c524_submitted-evidence-03-plaintiff-be7a5dd8c524; Reuters Connect public image-caption/metadata extraction ev_58561d19c453_submitted-evidence-04-plaintiff-58561d19c453. Reuters Connect page provides image caption: document including line about choosing one's time to say goodbye, described as suicide note purportedly written by Epstein, seen after release by Judge Karas in NYC May 6, 2026; source U.S. District Court/SDNY handout via Reuters; USN RC2T3LAL0C66; dimensions 2550x1700/741.2KB; page says image processed by Reuters and unprocessed version separately provided. Adverse checks preserved: SDNY order grants public access and identifies the note as purported, but does not authenticate authorship. PBS/AP says unclear who wrote note, not in government reports/DOJ release, and reports Epstein said Tartaglione tried to kill him. Guardian says no signature and had not verified authorship; Epstein maintained not suicidal. Filing theory: under market rule, plaintiff need not prove actual authorship; needs credible reporting that note was written by Epstein and intended as suicide/final/equivalent. Multiple independent credible sources and court/source metadata before May 8 satisfy public availability and credible reporting by preponderance. Caveats go to actual-authorship certainty, not the proposition's reporting standard. Missing gaps: no binary court PDF admitted, no actual note image download admitted, no full NYT capture, no full Reuters article text; but admitted official order/extract and Reuters image metadata plus AP/Guardian are sufficient.

Arguments:defendant work notes. Re-scanned current record with get_case/list_evidence. Plaintiff added four evidence extracts and a merits argument: SDNY order extraction ev_0ed0ef50e84e; PBS/AP ev_757899113d89; Guardian ev_be7a5dd8c524; Reuters Connect caption ev_58561d19c453. Read full byte ranges for all four. Assessment: public availability likely established; decisive dispute is whether there was preponderance/credible-reporting consensus on Epstein authorship and suicide/final-message intent. New plaintiff evidence contains adverse limits: SDNY order uses alleged/purported and is a public-access order; Reuters Connect says third-party supplied image processed by Reuters; PBS/AP says unclear who wrote note, not in government reports/files, Epstein accused Tartaglione; Guardian says no signature and no independent verification. Native investigation targeted direct AP/DOJ source-chain facts, NYT authentication follow-up, CBS/WaPo mirrors, Mark Epstein forgery claim. Found AP direct article at https://apnews.com/article/jeffrey-epstein-suicide-note-5297f49736e625a06d0db3c7b737b997. Submitted AP relevant extraction as defense evidence; accepted as ev_5aa56aab2530_submitted-evidence-05-defendant-5aa56aab2530. It preserves DOJ explanation that it never had the note, note not authenticated, first time DOJ saw it, note came through unrelated Tartaglione attorney-client dispute and court vault since 2021, and DOJ lawyers could not disclose sealed proceedings. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md. Filing plan: concede public release; argue plaintiff has not shown consensus credible reporting of authorship or intent; offer defense AP evidence plus key plaintiff exhibits for their limiting content.

Rebuttal opportunity notes. Rechecked current record and evidence list; new defendant evidence is ev_5aa56aab2530_submitted-evidence-05-defendant-5aa56aab2530. Read/statted exact item. Defense concedes public release before May 8 but argues source chain/authentication/intent defeat proposition. DB-1/AP reports DOJ never had the note, note not authenticated, first time DOJ seeing it, note came through Tartaglione attorney-client/conflict proceedings, and DOJ lawyers in sealed proceedings could not disclose. It also confirms note made public as part of unrelated case, NYT petition, Judge Karas agreed, DOJ did not object, and AP gives reported note text. Rebuttal theory: no new evidence needed; record is sufficient. Defendant's AP item is adverse to actual authorship certainty but not to the proposition's credible-reporting standard. Market/source rule in PX-1 asks consensus credible reporting; PX-4/PX-5/PX-6/PX-7 and DB-1 show mainstream qualified reporting and public release. Rebuttal should avoid repeating merits wholesale and target the defense's conversion of 'credibly reported' into 'officially authenticated.' Intent rebuttal: goodbye line plus context and repeated suicide-note characterization satisfies final message/equivalent; defense's ambiguity argument goes to actual intent certainty, not credible reporting by preponderance.

Surrebuttals:defendant notes. Re-scanned current record with get_case/list_evidence. Plaintiff filed rebuttal but submitted no new source evidence; evidence list unchanged except plaintiff re-offered defendant AP evidence as PX-R1. Rebuttal point: plaintiff says defense changes standard to actual authorship certainty and that alleged/purported reporting suffices under credible-reporting rule. Analysis: no new outside research needed because record evidence already resolves the narrow response. Surrebuttal will concede public release and that final authentication is not required, then distinguish credible reporting of a claim/purported note from credible reporting that the note was written by Epstein. DB-1/PX-R1 is not merely contextual; it supplies contrary official source-chain facts: DOJ never had the note, note not authenticated, first time DOJ saw it, sealed Tartaglione-attorney proceedings, and origin through Tartaglione/lawyers/court vault. Intent remains uncertain because plaintiff leans on one line while record preserves short/hard-to-decipher note and ambiguous context. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md. No new evidence planned.

Closing opportunity notes. Rechecked get_case/list_evidence. No new evidence after defendant AP item; new record includes defendant surrebuttal. Defendant surrebuttal repeats that credible reporting cannot equal reports that the note was alleged/purported when source chain is weak; insists authorship and intent elements remain unproved. Closing strategy: synthesize only admitted record. Lead with concession/public availability: defense concedes document publicly released before May 8. Decisive standard: PX-1 requires consensus credible reporting, not official authentication. Evidence: PX-4 SDNY order identifies request for suicide note purportedly authored by Epstein/recovered by Tartaglione/submitted by attorneys and grants public access; PX-5 Reuters/SDNY handout metadata dates release May 6 and includes goodbye line; PX-6 AP/PBS and PX-7 Guardian provide independent timely reporting and quote/context; DB/PX-R1 AP is adverse on authentication but confirms note made public, NYT petition, Karas agreement, DOJ non-objection, reported text, and public interest. Strongest defense point: DOJ never had it/not authenticated/source chain weak. Response: those caveats prevent actual-authorship certainty but do not defeat proposition's narrower credible-reporting standard. If official authentication were required, the rule's 'credibly reported' and 'resolution source consensus credible reporting' language would be meaningless. Closing should ask council to answer yes by preponderance while acknowledging caveats.

Closings:defendant notes. After ready prompt, re-scanned current record with get_case/list_evidence; no new evidence or changed evidence metadata since surrebuttal, and no evidence submission permitted in closing. Plaintiff closing argues the proposition is satisfied because qualified credible reporting of a purported/alleged note is enough, and that defense caveats only avoid saying Epstein definitely wrote it. Closing response plan: concede public release/timing; focus on plaintiff lowering the proposition from 'credibly reported to have been written by Epstein and intended as a suicide/final-message note' to 'reported as a purported/alleged Epstein note.' Decisive record: PX-4 is public-access order with alleged/purported framing, not authentication; PX-5 is third-party supplied/processed Reuters caption, not authentication; PX-6/PX-7 preserve unclear author, no government reports/files, no signature, no independent verification; DB-1/PX-R1 says DOJ never had note, it was not authenticated, DOJ saw it first time, and it came through sealed Tartaglione attorney-client proceedings/court vault. Closing ask: no, because plaintiff proved public availability of a disputed document, not the full qualifying proposition by preponderance. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.
